title: Questions for Trav — empty-body intent note (Oct 2023)
html:
TL;DR

  • Apple Note from October 26, 2023 titled "Questions for trav:" with a completely empty body — Yahir opened the note to draft questions for Travis, never typed any.
  • Confirms Travis remained a conversation partner / sought-counsel figure into late 2023, ~4 years after the Grants Pass teen era mentions.
  • Marks Travis's 4th in-corpus reference (after 2019 09 25 feeding illusion or ending it, 2019 09 30 show dad all of the documents, 2019 10 08 chinese practice) — the <a href="#" class="mind-link" data-node-id="entities/travis">travis</a> stub is well past the 3-mention promotion threshold and should be densified in the next pass. Flagging here, not promoting in this slice.
  • Pure empty-body subtype, asking the right question-adjacent: Yahir was preparing to ask, the asking didn't happen (or happened verbally with no notes).
  • October 26 2023 sits in a quiet stretch between the Jordan trip reflection and the November Beer Ball note.

Stub-promotion flag

  • <a href="#" class="mind-link" data-node-id="entities/travis">travis</a> is at 4 mentions, still tagged #stub. Per CLAUDE.md schema v2 rule, this should have been densified at mention 3. Densify-pass candidate. I am not promoting in this slice (lean batch), but logging the violation for the next densify operator.

Why an empty note matters

  • Same logic as 2023 07 28 speak things into existencen: the title-as-intent is the signal. Yahir's titling pattern alone tells the story even when the body never lands.
  • "Questions for X" is one of the cleanest search-friendly title formats in his corpus — easy to grep, easy to find later.

Connections

  • travis — promote pending
  • — empty-body subtype
  • asking the right question — adjacent concept
  • grants pass teen era — origin context for the Travis relationship
